name: Ohio State University Extension
description_short: Ohio State University Extension's Family and Consumer Sciences program offers free educational resources and workshops on financial management, homebuying, nutrition, and family wellness.
description_long:
Ohio State University Extension (OSUE) is a statewide public education initiative operated by The Ohio State University College of Agriculture and Consumer Sciences (CFAES). With roots in land-grant university outreach, it operates as a non-profit educational service rather than a commercial lender or credit repair service. The organization delivers evidence-based resources directly to Ohio residents through county-based educators, online content, and community workshops. Family and Consumer Sciences (FCS) is one of several program areas within OSUE that specifically addresses financial literacy, housing, nutrition, and family support topics.

OSUE's offerings include HUD-approved homebuyer education workshops (scheduled quarterly), nutrition and grocery budgeting guidance from certified nutritionists, Alzheimer's and dementia caregiver support programs, food safety training for community volunteers, and aging support webinars. The organization also runs the Live Smart Ohio initiative and maintains an events calendar with public workshops on topics ranging from homeownership preparation to financial wellness. All programming appears to be free or low-cost, consistent with the educational mission of a land-grant institution.

OSUE distinguishes itself through its university affiliation and research-backed curricula. Rather than offering quick fixes or commercial products, the organization provides comprehensive educational programs developed by faculty experts. Their HUD-approved homebuyer education status indicates compliance with federal housing standards. The statewide network of county Extension educators means localized access to programming. As a public education entity, there are no profit motives, hidden fees, or product sales underlying their recommendations.

The primary limitation is that OSUE offers education and referrals rather than direct financial services. They cannot provide credit counseling with debt negotiation components, debt settlement, loan products, or credit repair dispute services. Their resources appear strongest for preventive financial education (budgeting, homebuying preparation, nutrition savings) rather than crisis intervention for those already in severe debt. Website navigation could be improved for consumers seeking specific financial services—many visitors may not immediately recognize OSUE as a financial assistance resource.
pros: HUD-approved homebuyer education workshops ensure curriculum meets federal housing standards
Free educational programming with no commercial product sales or hidden fees
University-backed content developed by credentialed nutritionists, educators, and nursing faculty
Statewide county Extension network provides local access to in-person workshops and educators
Covers practical financial topics: grocery budgeting, homebuying preparation, caregiver financial planning
Established dementia caregiver support through Golden Buckeye Center partnership with Ohio Department of Aging
Regular quarterly homebuyer workshops (4/9, 5/2 dates visible) with consistent scheduling
cons: Does not offer direct credit counseling, debt negotiation, or credit repair services
Website does not clearly explain which programs are free vs. fee-based or any associated costs
Limited information on financial crisis support—appears to focus on prevention rather than debt relief
No visible NFCC certification or credit counselor credentials mentioned for financial guidance staff
Educational model means help is delayed by workshop scheduling rather than immediate access
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking HUD-approved education before mortgage application
Families managing grocery budgets and seeking nutrition/financial wellness education
Adult children and family members caregiving for aging relatives or dementia patients
Ohio residents wanting free, research-backed financial literacy resources without sales pressure
diagnosis: Best for Ohio residents seeking free, judgment-free financial education on homebuying, budgeting, and family caregiving from a credible university source. Primary caveat: OSUE is educational, not a credit counseling or debt relief agency—those needing active debt negotiation or credit repair should be referred elsewhere.
services: Grocery budgeting and food cost reduction guidance
Nutrition education and meal planning resources
Alzheimer's and dementia caregiver support programs
Golden Buckeye Center for Dementia Caregivers partnership and education
Food safety training (Occasional Quantity Cooks curriculum)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
